meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by deploying a high-velocity SaaS starter kit. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid MVP production.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a perception issue, not a capability one. By leveraging a high-quality SaaS starter kit, you shift from building from scratch to configuring for value. This allows you to ship a polished, production-ready MVP in hours, effectively demonstrating technical execution and business acumen to potential clients.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the graveyard of ambition. You have the vision, but your GitHub profile is a barren landscape, and the 'no portfolio' stigma keeps the high-ticket clients at bay. You aren't just missing code; you are missing the signal of authority that only a deployed, functional SaaS product can provide.

Stop waiting for the perfect project to fall into your lap. We are going to shortcut the process by deploying a hardened SaaS starter kit. By combining the infrastructure power of Antigravity with the autonomous reasoning of Claude Code, you will transform from a 'developer with no work' into a 'founder with a live asset' in a single afternoon.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundation of your SaaS using a battle-tested stack. We prioritize authentication, database connectivity, and billing logic immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, production-ready SaaS boilerplate using Next.js 14, Supabase for auth/DB, and Stripe integration. Ensure the directory structure follows industry best practices for scalability.

steps.title: Automate Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models to support user-specific multi-tenancy, which is the hallmark of a professional SaaS application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust SQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for users, organizations, subscriptions, and usage-based features.

steps.title: Integrate Identity and Auth
steps.description: Implement secure authentication flows. Clients trust founders who prioritize user data protection from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Supabase Auth providers for Google and Email/Password with middleware protection for authenticated routes.

steps.title: Accelerate UI/UX with Component Libraries
steps.description: Use high-conversion UI components to ensure your MVP looks like a Series-A funded product, not a student project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a landing page and dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS and Shadcn/UI components that emphasize conversion-led design.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your code to a live environment. A URL is the ultimate proof of work for any founder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure CI/CD pipelines for Vercel deployment and set up environment variables for production readiness.

pro_tips: Never build custom auth; use managed services like Supabase or Clerk to save 40+ hours of security engineering.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Payment' over feature bloat; if it doesn't help the user pay, delete it.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests concurrently with the features; it creates a 'professional-grade' commit history.
Documentation is your secret marketing tool; write a clean README that explains the 'why' behind your architecture.
Always include a 'Request Feature' button in your MVP; it turns passive users into active stakeholders immediately.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 24 hours focusing on 'marketing as code'—documenting your build process on X or LinkedIn to attract your first users.
content.conversion_block: You now have a live, professional-grade SaaS product to showcase. The 'no portfolio' excuse is officially dead. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
